```java
package com.example.service;

import java.security.MessageDigest;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;

/**
 * Service class for computing message digests (hash values) of file content.
 * Uses SHA-256 algorithm for secure and efficient hashing.
 */
public class HashService {
    
    /**
     * Computes the SHA-256 hash of the given byte array and returns it as a hexadecimal string.
     *
     * @param data the byte array representing the file content
     * @return the hexadecimal string representation of the hash
     * @throws RuntimeException if the SHA-256 algorithm is not supported
     */
    public String digest(byte[] data) {
        try {
            // Create SHA-256 message digest instance
            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
            
            // Compute the hash value
            byte[] hashBytes = digest.digest(data);
            
            // Convert byte array to hexadecimal string
            return bytesToHex(hashBytes);
            
        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
            // Wrap checked exception in unchecked exception as per Java security API
            throw new RuntimeException("SHA-256 algorithm is not supported", e);
        }
    }
    
    /**
     * Converts a byte array to its hexadecimal string representation.
     *
     * @param bytes the byte array to convert
     * @return hexadecimal string representation
     */
    private String bytesToHex(byte[] bytes) {
        StringBuilder hexString = new StringBuilder();
        for (byte b : bytes) {
            String hex = Integer.toHexString(0xff & b);
            if (hex.length() == 1) {
                hexString.append('0');
            }
            hexString.append(hex);
        }
        return hexString.toString();
    }
}
```

```java
package com.example.service;

import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
import java.security.MessageDigest;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;

/**
 * Service class for computing message digests (hash values) of file content.
 * Uses SHA-256 algorithm to generate secure hash values.
 */
public class HashService {
    
    private static final String ALGORITHM = "SHA-256";
    
    /**
     * Computes the SHA-256 hash of the given data and returns it as a hexadecimal string.
     *
     * @param data the byte array containing the file content to hash
     * @return the hexadecimal string representation of the hash
     * @throws RuntimeException if the SHA-256 algorithm is not supported by the system
     */
    public String digest(byte[] data) {
        if (data == null) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Input data cannot be null");
        }
        
        try {
            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance(ALGORITHM);
            byte[] hashBytes = digest.digest(data);
            return bytesToHex(hashBytes);
        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Hash algorithm " + ALGORITHM + " is not supported", e);
        }
    }
    
    /**
     * Converts a byte array to its hexadecimal string representation.
     *
     * @param bytes the byte array to convert
     * @return the hexadecimal string representation
     */
    private String bytesToHex(byte[] bytes) {
        StringBuilder hexString = new StringBuilder(2 * bytes.length);
        for (byte b : bytes) {
            String hex = Integer.toHexString(0xff & b);
            if (hex.length() == 1) {
                hexString.append('0');
            }
            hexString.append(hex);
        }
        return hexString.toString();
    }
}
```

```java
package com.example.service;

import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
import java.security.MessageDigest;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;

/**
 * Service class for computing message digests (hash values) of file contents.
 * Uses SHA-256 algorithm for secure and efficient hashing.
 */
public class HashService {
    
    private static final String ALGORITHM = "SHA-256";
    private static final String HEX_CHARS = "0123456789ABCDEF";
    
    /**
     * Computes the SHA-256 hash of the provided data and returns it as a hexadecimal string.
     *
     * @param data the byte array representing the file content
     * @return the hexadecimal representation of the hash
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if data is null
     * @throws RuntimeException if the hashing algorithm is not supported
     */
    public String digest(byte[] data) {
        if (data == null) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Input data cannot be null");
        }
        
        try {
            // Get SHA-256 message digest instance
            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance(ALGORITHM);
            
            // Compute the hash
            byte[] hashBytes = digest.digest(data);
            
            // Convert to hexadecimal string
            return bytesToHex(hashBytes);
            
        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
            // This should never happen as SHA-256 is a standard algorithm
            throw new RuntimeException("Hash algorithm " + ALGORITHM + " is not supported", e);
        }
    }
    
    /**
     * Converts a byte array to a hexadecimal string.
     *
     * @param bytes the byte array to convert
     * @return hexadecimal string representation
     */
    private String bytesToHex(byte[] bytes) {
        if (bytes == null || bytes.length == 0) {
            return "";
        }
        
        char[] hexChars = new char[bytes.length * 2];
        for (int i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) {
            int v = bytes[i] & 0xFF;
            hexChars[i * 2] = HEX_CHARS.charAt(v >>> 4);
            hexChars[i * 2 + 1] = HEX_CHARS.charAt(v & 0x0F);
        }
        return new String(hexChars);
    }
}
```

```java
package com.example.service;

import java.security.MessageDigest;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;

/**
 * Service class for computing file message digests (hash values).
 * Uses SHA-256 algorithm for secure and efficient hashing.
 */
public class HashService {

    /**
     * Computes the SHA-256 hash of the given data and returns it as a hexadecimal string.
     *
     * @param data the input byte array to hash
     * @return the hexadecimal representation of the hash
     * @throws IllegalStateException if SHA-256 algorithm is not available
     * @throws NullPointerException if data is null
     */
    public String digest(byte[] data) {
        try {
            // Initialize SHA-256 message digest
            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
            
            // Compute hash of input data
            byte[] hashBytes = digest.digest(data);
            
            // Convert bytes to hexadecimal string
            return bytesToHex(hashBytes);
        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
            // Re-throw as unchecked exception since SHA-256 is guaranteed in Java 8+
            throw new IllegalStateException("SHA-256 algorithm not available", e);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Converts a byte array to a hexadecimal string.
     *
     * @param bytes the byte array to convert
     * @return hexadecimal string representation
     * @throws NullPointerException if bytes is null
     */
    private String bytesToHex(byte[] bytes) {
        StringBuilder hexString = new StringBuilder();
        for (byte b : bytes) {
            String hex = Integer.toHexString(0xff & b);
            if (hex.length() == 1) {
                hexString.append('0');
            }
            hexString.append(hex);
        }
        return hexString.toString();
    }
}
```
